commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs maintainer judgment on the new persistent marker scope plus contributor real behavior proof; there is no narrow autonomous repair target after the stale-marker fix.; Cleared: The live PR patch touches session state, command parsing, and tests only; it adds no dependency, workflow, secret, download, or external code-execution surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in `--persist` flag for `/usage`, `/thinking`/`/think`, and `/model`, plus a stored `persistentPreferenceFields` session-entry marker used during rollover.

PR surface: Source +206, Tests +293. Total +499 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main drops `responseUsage` on stale rollover while preserving other session preferences, and PR head adds marker-gated carry-forward plus tests for persisted and non-persisted usage. I did not run tests because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer judgment on the new persistent marker scope plus contributor real behavior proof; there is no narrow autonomous repair target after the stale-marker fix.

target.title: feat(reply): persist session preferences
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds explicit `--persist` support for reply preferences that users may want to survive session rollover:

- `/usage <mode> --persist`
- `/thinking <level> --persist` / `/think <level> --persist`
- `/model <provider/model> --persist`

Persistent intent is tracked with `persistentPreferenceFields`, so rollover can distinguish durable user preferences from ordinary session-local overrides.

## What Problem This Solves

Some reply preferences currently behave like transcript-local session state. When a session rolls over after a daily or idle boundary, settings such as `/usage full` disappear even when users intended them as durable preferences.

The explicit flag keeps existing behavior safe by default: preferences remain temporary unless the user opts in with `--persist`.

## Behavior

- Persisted `/usage` values survive daily and idle session rollover.
- Non-persisted `/usage` values continue to be dropped on rollover.
- `/model` and `/thinking` acknowledge and record the same persistence intent consistently with `/usage`.
- `--persist` is stripped from agent-visible directive text.
- Ordinary user messages containing `--persist` are not rewritten.
- A later non-`--persist` update clears the stale marker for that field, so `/usage tokens` after `/usage full --persist` becomes temporary again instead of accidentally surviving the next rollover.

## Evidence

Real behavior covered by focused tests:

- `/usage full --persist` writes `responseUsage: "full"` plus `persistentPreferenceFields: ["responseUsage"]`.
- Daily rollover preserves persisted `responseUsage`.
- Daily rollover drops non-persisted `responseUsage`, preserving the existing default behavior.
- `/think high --persist` and `/model openai/gpt-4o --persist` store persistent markers and strip `--persist` from agent-visible text.
- Non-persistent follow-up updates clear stale markers for `/usage`, `/think`, and `/model`, including mixed-content directive paths.
- Ordinary messages containing `--persist` remain unchanged.

Latest validation on current head `4edfd5b13a8ecb317546e786faa0a21d7e2e0666`:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts
```

Result: 3 test files passed, 207 tests passed.

```bash
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.impl.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.persist.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Result: all passed.

Additional validation previously run on this branch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply.directive.parse.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

Result: 4 related test files passed, 240 tests passed, and extensions typecheck passed.

## Related Issues

Related: #17102, #12578
